In '92, 5-9 Iowa State got in. There is some nostalgic idea that only the best P6 teams used to get in. But it's not true. Mediocre P6 teams got in, there were just fewer of them available so plenty of room for the best MM at-larges. That is no longer the case. Need to expand. https://t.co/JvxHZ25I8Q

What's changed is there's less access for MM at-larges. Expanding the tourney helps with that. Anti-expansion = anti-Robbie Avila.

(On a percentage basis, I'd bet that fewer sub-.500 P5 teams get in now than in the 80s but one of you nerds should graph it up and report back.)

Access has improved for P5 teams as a whole because there are more of them. Creighton, Butler, Houston, BYU  are now P5. It hasn't changed for individual teams. 

16-12 Kentucky made the '85 tourney. 18-13 (6-8) Maryland was a 5-seed(!) in 86. 17-12 (6-8) Maryland was a 7 in '88 https://t.co/oJtORbHGhK

In 1985, 64 (22.8%) of 280 teams made the tourney. In 2024, 68 (19.4%) of 351 teams made it. The tournament is more exclusive than it's been since 1984. 

Nothing's going to lose meaning by adding a few teams. https://t.co/gTEUAMv2Ft

RIP my mentions, but 76 teams is totally responsible expansion and I don't understand why people are so hostile to it. It would have been cool to have had Indiana State in last season's tourney. So what if it comes with St. John's and Oklahoma joining them.

At Zach Edey's current pace, if he plays 40 games (to title game), he's on pace to shoot 444 FT's, which would tie the NCAA record set by Frank Selvy in 1954. In '54, there were a whopping 32.2 FTA's shot per team/game. This year, 19.0. Just an absurd outlier of a season.
